proof: [§6] With respect to the different ages, children are frequently affected, but do not often die, because the affection is familiar and akin to them;
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author identifies children as a group that, despite high infection rates, possesses a systematic biological and customary 'affinity' to the disease that prevents fatality, effectively granting them a higher survival rate compared to other age groups.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to a large demographic (all children) within the context of a specific disease.

proof: [§6] old men are most subject to the disease, and most apt to die; the cause of this is the frigidity and dryness of old age, and the nature of the death.

extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the senior male population.

proof: [§6] Women are more disposed to tetanus than men, because they are of a cold temperament; but they more readily recover, because they are of a humid.

extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This affects half the population (women) across the society.

proof: [§6] And women also suffer from this spasm after abortion; and, in this case, they seldom recover.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text identifies a specific immutable state (post-abortion female status) where the legal/biological consequence is a near-certain loss of life when afflicted by spasms.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to women only in the specific circumstance following an abortion.

proof: [§6] striplings are less liable to suffer, but more readily die;

extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies to the specific age group of late childhood/early youth.

proof: [§9] wherefore children, until puberty, especially suffer, for children in particular have large and cold respiration; for there is most heat in them; moreover, they are intemperate in regard to food
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author notes that children until puberty are systematically more prone to dangerous throat ulcerations due to their natural physical and behavioral traits.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies to all prepubescent children.

proof: [§9] and these diseases are familiar to girls until they have their menstrual purgation.

extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies specifically to prepubescent females.

proof: [§10] With regard to age, old men are most apt to suffer, and most readily escape from an attack; for neither is there apt to be a great inflammation in an arid frame;
extracted_rules.reasoning: In contrast to tetanus, the author states that the nature of old age (aridity/frigidity) provides a systematic protection against death from pleurisy.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies to the elderly male demographic.

proof: [§10] Children are least of all liable to pleurisy, and in their case it is less frequently fatal; for their bodies are rare, secretions copious, perspiration and exhalation abundant;
extracted_rules.reasoning: The biological status of childhood ('rare' bodies and high perspiration) is cited as a rule-like condition that protects them from both contracting and dying from pleurisy.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: Applies to all children in the population.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to customary medical assistance during paroxysm
proof: [§2] But those to whom the affection has become habitual, whenever the disease recurs, and has already seized the finger, or is commencing in any part, having from experience a foreknowledge of what is about to happen, call, from among those who are present, upon their customary assistants, and entreat them to bind, pull aside, and stretch the affected members.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text describes a socially recognized practice where individuals with chronic epilepsy have 'customary assistants' who are expected to perform specific physical interventions (binding and stretching limbs) to mitigate or delay the onset of a seizure.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies specifically to a subset of the population defined by a chronic medical condition (habitual epilepsy) rather than the general population.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to perform corrective surgery on living patients
proof: [§3] But neither can the physician, though present and looking on, furnish any assistance, as regards life, relief from pain or from deformity. For if he should wish to straighten the limbs, he can only do so by cutting and breaking those of a living man.

proof: [§6] wherefore children, until puberty, especially suffer, for children in particular have large and cold respiration; for there is most heat in them; moreover, they are intemperate in regard to food, have a longing for varied food and cold drink; and they bawl loud both in anger and in sport
extracted_rules.reasoning: Aretaeus identifies children as a group systematically deprived of protection from fatal tonsil ulcerations due to their inherent biological heat and social behaviors (shouting, dietary habits), treating this as a fixed rule of their age group.

proof: [§6] The land of Egypt especially engenders it, the air thereof being dry for respiration, and the food diversified, consisting of roots, herbs of many kinds, acrid seeds, and thick drink; namely, the water of the Nile, and the sort of ale prepared from barley. Syria also, and more especially Cœlosyria, engenders these diseases
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text identifies people in Egypt and Syria as being systematically more exposed to (and less protected from) fatal diseases due to their local environment, water source, and diet, creating a rule of geographic stratification regarding bodily safety.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to entire regional populations.

proof: [§7] With regard to age, old men are most apt to suffer, and most readily escape from an attack; for neither is there apt to be a great inflammation in an arid frame; nor is there a metastasis to the lungs, for old age is more frigid than any other age, and the respiration small, and the attraction of all things deficient.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The author identifies a specific demographic (old men) who, while susceptible to the disease, possess a systematic biological/status advantage in surviving it compared to other age groups. In Greco-Roman medicine, 'escaping' an attack refers to the reduced fatality rate attributed to their specific physiological 'arid' and 'frigid' state.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: The rule applies to a broad biological age class (elderly males) within the population.

proof: [§7] Young men and adults are not, indeed, very apt to suffer attacks; but neither, also, do they readily recover, for from a slight cause they would not experience even a slight attack of inflammation, and from great attacks there is greater danger.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text explicitly states that young/adult men have a harder time recovering from pleurisy than old men or children. This constitutes a systematic loss of the 'resource' of survivability/convalescence for this specific age group in the context of this disease.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to the prime-age male population, a significant but specific slice of society.

proof: [§8] This mode of bringing up blood is common with women who have not their monthly purgation, and appears at the periods of the purgation, and stops during the intervals between them; and if the woman is not cured, the discharge upwards of blood will revert for many periods, and also, in certain cases, the vessels burst from fulness.
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text describes a specific medical vulnerability where women lacking regular menstruation suffer from a systematic diversion of blood to the lungs or chest (bringing up blood), which the author treats as a recurring societal/medical rule for this group.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: It specifically targets women of childbearing age who experience amenorrhea, a significant but specific sub-group.

extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to a broad demographic category (youths and adults) within the society.

proof: [§11] ...whereas in cardiac affections the patients are more acute in their senses, so that they see and hear better than formerly; they are also in understanding more sound, and in mind more pure, not only regarding present things, but also with regard to futurity they are true prophets.

[§12] As to the state of the soul, every sense is pure, the intellect acute, the gnostic powers prophetic; for they prognosticate to themselves, in the first place, their own departure from life; then they foretell what will afterwards take place to those present, who fancy sometimes that they are delirious; but these persons wonder at the result of what has been said.
extracted_rules.reasoning: Aretaeus describes a specific social and intellectual status granted to those dying of cardiac syncope: they are regarded not as delirious, but as having 'pure' minds and 'gnostic powers' that make them 'true prophets'. This implies a societal rule where the speech of the dying is elevated to a higher level of truth-value than that of healthy persons.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This applies to a specific subset of the population (the dying/critically ill) under specific medical conditions.

proof: [§19] For this reason the affection occurs in young women, but not in old.
